channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Walz tramps through tall grass on Minnesota's pheasant hunting season opener but bags no birds
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — Democratic vice presidential candidate Tim Walz spent Saturday morning tramping through tall grass on the opening day of Minnesota's pheasant hunting season, giving the campaign a chance to highlight the governor's rural roots and love of outdoor sports. Neither Walz nor Lt. Gov.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Doctor deems Harris in 'excellent health.' Her team aims to contrast with Trump's scant medical info
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris is in “excellent health” and “possesses the physical and mental resiliency” required to serve as president, her doctor said in a letter released Saturday that summarizes her medical history and status. Dr.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump hears at a Latino campaign event from someone who lived in the US illegally
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Minutes after Donald Trump delivered his standard warnings about drug dealers and criminals illegally crossing the border during a Saturday campaign event, the former president heard from someone who was once in the country illegally but now plans to vote for him.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: America First Policy Institute, a group advising Trump, says its systems were breached
item.description: A group helping to lay the groundwork for a future Donald Trump administration said its computer systems were breached, marking the second known instance that people supporting the former president have been the target of a cyberattack.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris-Walz campaign targets hunters, fishermen for support
item.description: The Harris-Walz campaign launched a new coalition designed to draw voter support from outdoorsmen in swing states
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Data shows migrants aren’t taking jobs from Black or Hispanic people, despite what Trump says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican presidential nominee Donald Trump promises the biggest deportation event the U.S. has ever seen if he is elected — a promise he has predicated, in part, on the notion that immigrants in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Fight for House raging in districts ringing NYC where Trump struggled but other Republicans thrived
item.description: Voters in a ring of congressional districts encircling New York City where Republican candidates often do well but Donald Trump struggled in 2020 could decide which party controls the U.S. House for the next two years.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Izaguirre, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Tameka Henry discusses oversight, safety, and leadership in Clark County School District race
item.description: Tameka Henry, a candidate for the Clark County Board of Trustees, appeared on the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide to discuss her platform and vision for improving the school district.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A federal judge rejects a call to reopen voter registration in Georgia after Hurricane Helene
item.description: A federal judge said Thursday that she won't order the presidential battleground state of Georgia to reopen voter registration for November’s elections despite recent disruptions to registration caused by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Interim NYPD Commissioner Tom Donlon could resign: sources
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Less than a month after the NYPD's interim commissioner was appointed, another major shake-up could be coming. Interim NYPD Commissioner Tom Donlon is expected to step down, sources said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, NewsNation

item.title: Trump leads Harris by 4 points in Florida: Survey
item.description: Former President Trump is leading Vice President Harris by a narrow 4 points in Florida, according to a new survey of likely voters in the state.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Pulpit politics: When does it cross the line?
item.description: The lead pastor at a metro Grand Rapids megachurch got political in a recent sermon, urging his parishioners to run from the Democratic party.
item.dc:creator: Ken Kolker, NewsNation

item.title: Harris accepts invite to CNN town hall as another Trump debate appears unlikely
item.description: Vice President Harris has accepted an invitation from CNN to participate in a town hall on the network a week before Election Day, as it appears unlikely that former President Trump will agree to a second debate with her.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Republican and Democratic House Latinos face off in race to increase their numbers
item.description: Latino House Republicans are building out campaign infrastructure to protect their members and grow their numbers, at times directly competing with their Democratic counterparts, who have a huge head start. Rep.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, NewsNation

item.title: Pat Ryan 'incredibly frustrated' over lack of FEMA relief in Orange County
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- The race for New York’s 18th Congressional District heated up on Wednesday evening as candidates Alison Esposito and Pat Ryan faced off in a live debate on PIX11 News.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, PIX 11

item.title: Candidate Kamilah Bywaters discusses her vision for the Clark County School Board
item.description: Kamilah Bywaters, a candidate for the Clark County School District (CCSD) Board of Trustees, joined 8 News Now’s Daily Voting Guide to discuss her plans and priorities for District E, which covers Summerlin and northwest parts of the Las Vegas Valley.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pat Ryan, Alison Esposito face off in NY-18 debate on PIX11
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Democratic U.S. Rep. Pat Ryan and Republican Alison Esposito, the two candidates running to represent New York's 18th Congressional District, faced off in a debate on PIX11 Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Mayor Frey vetoes carbon emission fee adopted by city council
item.description: In his veto letter Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey said he agrees with the aims of the fee on polluters, but he said the council’s action amounts to an “unauthorized tax.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: Brown says no to pro-Palestinian students' demands for divestment
item.description: Supporters of divestment ended their encampment last spring in exchange for a promise that their proposal for divestment would get a vote from the board this fall.
item.dc:creator: Tovia Smith, MPR News

item.title: Florida has nearly all ballots counted on Election Day, while California can take weeks. This is why
item.description: Why is it that some states have nearly all the ballots counted within hours of polls closing while others take weeks?
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: Candidates for Las Vegas Mayor face off in a debate ahead of Election Day
item.description: Las Vegas Mayoral candidates Shelley Berkley and Victoria Seaman will face off in a debate on Tuesday, hosted by 8 News Now, with the winner taking over the non-partisan office from Oscar Goodman, who served as mayor from 1999 to 2011.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Harris, Walz appeared on ABC's 'The View' and 'Jimmy Kimmel Live'
item.description: The Democratic presidential ticket is on a media blitz in New York. Vice President Kamala Harris appeared Tuesday on ABC's “The View," and her running mate, Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz appeared on ABC's “Jimmy Kimmel Live” on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: U.S. Treasury says tribally owned entities should be tax-exempt
item.description: Proposed new guidelines would clarify the decades-old question of whether tribal businesses or entities are exempt from federal taxes.
item.dc:creator: MPR News

item.title: Hurricane Milton may be October surprise in presidential battle
item.description: Strategists in both parties are looking back to Hurricane Sandy, which slammed into the mid-Atlantic states shortly before Election Day 2012.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Your guide to voting in Minnesota: Registration, absentee ballots and general election details
item.description: We are less than a month out from Election Day. Here are answers to some frequently asked questions MPR News readers asked us about the upcoming election.
item.dc:creator: Anika Besst and Nicole Johnson, MPR News

item.title: Harris is pressed for more specifics in a wide-ranging interview with '60 Minutes'
item.description: The interview followed criticism from former President Donald Trump that Kamala Harris has been unwilling to sit for hard interviews. She was pressed on everything from her economic proposals to immigration.
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk, MPR News

item.title: Michael Naft discusses traffic safety, F1, and animal welfare in the Las Vegas valley
item.description: Michael Naft, incumbent county commissioner for District 8, joined Brian Loftis on the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide Monday, discussing his campaign priorities as election day nears.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Supreme Court won’t hear Pennsylvania legislators' challenge to executive actions expanding voting access
item.description: The Supreme Court decided Monday it won’t take up a case to restore a lawsuit by Pennsylvania Republicans that challenged executive actions expanding voting access on the basis that only state legislatures can regulate federal elections.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court won't hear arguments in Texas emergency abortion case
item.description: The Supreme Court decided not to hear arguments in a case involving Texas that could have provided an answer about whether a state abortion ban conflicts with a federal emergency care law.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, Fox 5

item.title: Minnesota Republicans seek a reset after two years of DFL trifecta in St. Paul
item.description: All 134 Minnesota House seats are on the ballot this fall, along with one state Senate seat. The results could flip political control of the Legislature.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: This year’s Electoral College map may show another ‘blue shift.’ Here’s why
item.description: Differences in how quickly each state’s counties can report this year’s election results may lead to another “blue shift” or “red mirage” on the presidential electoral map.
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang, MPR News
